issue.title: [Feature Request] Add isMalicious.com as a data source
issue.body:
## Feature Request

I'd like to suggest adding [isMalicious.com](https://ismalicious.com) as a data source for your blocklists.

## About isMalicious

isMalicious is a threat intelligence platform that aggregates malicious domain data from multiple reputable sources. It provides regularly updated blocklists categorized by threat type.

## Available Blocklists

| List | URL | Description |
|------|-----|-------------|
| All Malicious | `https://ismalicious.com/api/blocklist/download/blocklist-domains-all.txt` | Combined list |
| Phishing | `https://ismalicious.com/api/blocklist/download/blocklist-domains-phishing.txt` | Phishing sites |
| Malware | `https://ismalicious.com/api/blocklist/download/blocklist-domains-malware.txt` | Malware distribution |
| Ransomware | `https://ismalicious.com/api/blocklist/download/blocklist-domains-ransomware.txt` | Ransomware-related |
| C2 | `https://ismalicious.com/api/blocklist/download/blocklist-domains-c2.txt` | Command & Control |
| Scam | `https://ismalicious.com/api/blocklist/download/blocklist-domains-scam.txt` | Scam/fraud sites |

## Format

Lists are plain text with one domain per line (no prefix):
```
malicious-domain1.com
phishing-site.net
ransomware-c2.org
```

## Why Add This Source?

- **Multi-source aggregation**: Combines data from multiple threat intelligence feeds
- **Categorized lists**: Separate lists for different threat types align with your category system
- **Daily updates**: Fresh threat data
- **Free/public access**: No authentication required for blocklist downloads
- **MIT Licensed**: Open source compatible

## Links

- Website: https://ismalicious.com
- Documentation: https://docs.ismalicious.com

Would the maintainers be interested in evaluating isMalicious as a source for the malware, phishing, ransomware, and/or scam lists?

Thank you for maintaining this excellent project!

issue.title: [Remove] verizonwireless.com
issue.body:
### Domain to remove

verizonwireless.com

### Current blocklist

phishing

### Why is this a false positive?

- This is a valid domain for verizon wireless that is owned by Verizon and redirects to verizon.com.

### Official website or documentation

_No response_

### Impact description

Cannot login to Verizon account.  Attempting to login uses https://secure.verizon.com which is a CNAME for login.verizonwireless.com which is currently blocked.


### Urgency

Medium - Affecting daily use

### Verification

- [x] I have verified this domain is currently being blocked by this project
- [x] I am not affiliated with the domain in a way that creates a conflict of interest
- [x] I understand removal requests are reviewed and may take time

issue.title: 0.0.0.0 does not work with IPv6.
issue.body:
If you are using IPv6 or a dual-stack (IPv4 + IPv6) configuration, 0.0.0.0 will not apply to IPv6 connections — you must also include **::**

**Example for dual-stack (IPv4 + IPv6):**

```
0.0.0.0 domain.tld
:: domain.tld
```

issue.title: [Remove Request] - Request to remove Nimiq related rules due to Proof-of-Stake migration
issue.body:
URL you wish to be removed:
```
nimiq.com
*.nimiq.com
nimiq.watch
```

Why you believe this to be a false positive:
Nimiq has transitioned fully to PoS (Nimiq 2.0) as of [19/11/2024](https://www.nimiq.com/blog/nimiq-proof-of-stake-is-now-live/), eliminating all Proof-of-Work (PoW) mining activities. The current rules in Easylist appear to block components critical to Nimiq’s node operations, particularly in web environments and mobile apps were users are using adblockers or VPNs. These components are essential for network participation (e.g., node synchronization and peer-to-peer communication) and are unrelated to cryptocurrency mining.

As Nimiq has fully transitioned to Proof of Stake (PoS), web-based mining is no longer part of its protocol. The existing rules blocking Nimiq related URLs can now be safely removed, as they only disrupt legitimate node operations.

List it is on:
```
ads
crypto
everything
```

Other info you think we should know:

https://github.com/easylist/easylist/issues/21276
https://github.com/easylist/easylist/issues/2618
https://github.com/hagezi/dns-blocklists/issues/5269
https://github.com/badmojr/1Hosts/issues/1896
https://github.com/mypdns/matrix/issues/109811
[https://github.com/Ultimate-Hosts-Black ... issues/221](https://github.com/Ultimate-Hosts-Blacklist/whitelist/issues/221)
[https://github.com/smed79/blacklist/com ... 78168fe735](https://github.com/smed79/blacklist/commit/60598cceaa51674285fb4e3184a49c78168fe735)
[https://github.com/hoshsadiq/adblock-no ... da61dddfc3](https://github.com/hoshsadiq/adblock-nocoin-list/commit/d8c301027e1816afe3c7cd2f6bf89ada61dddfc3)
[https://github.com/easylist/listefr/com ... 23507b985c](https://github.com/easylist/listefr/commit/ee4129fbed97fd617fd40f01829e6e23507b985c)

issue.title: [Remove Request] - mytaxexpress.com
issue.body:
URL you wish to be removed:  mytaxexpress.com


Why you believe this to be a false positive:

Is a legit Canadian Tax software company, certified by the CRA (Canadian Revenue Agency), as listed here --> https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/e-services/digital-services-individuals/netfile-overview/certified-software-netfile-program.html


List it is on:

https://raw.githubusercontent.com/blocklistproject/Lists/master/malware.txt


Other info you think we should know:

They asked off the MalwareBytes list here --> https://forums.malwarebytes.com/topic/325090-false-positive-mytaxexpresscom/
